title: anagallis serpens subsp. meyeri-johannis
intro: Anagallis serpens subsp. meyeri-johannis, also known as the Meyer's anagallis or yellow pimpernel, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Primulaceae. This plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Anagallis serpens subsp. meyeri-johannis include its ability to aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its expectorant and diaphoretic properties. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
